etymology.text: Verbal noun of عَدُلَ (ʕadula) and عَدَلَ (ʕadala).

body.text: , in two places. It is an inf. n. of
(S, O, Msb) said of a witness; like ↓
quality of a witness such as is termed
[q. v.]: it is expl. as being
a quality the regard of which necessitates the guarding against what falls short of the requirements of manly virtue
moral goodness, habitually and evidently; which evident falling short thereof is not effected by small instances of lapses
falls into wrongdoing, and by perversion of speech, because mistake and forgetfulness are supposable
and interpretation not according to the obvious meaning; but it is when such is the known and repeated practice of the person: regard is to be had to the goodness,

honesty, of every individual, and his usual practice in respect of his apparel, and his dealing in selling and buying, and the conveyance of goods, and other things; and when he does that which is not suitable to him, without necessity, his testimony is impugned; otherwise it is not.
